commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Edge cases like empty data or invalid authorization slip through
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Include negative tests, parameter boundary tests, and authorization variations

commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Row-level security defects surface in production audits
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Test DCL with multiple user IDs across role combinations in ADT preview

commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: $filter or $expand may fail at runtime even if SQL works in ADT
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Run scenarios in /IWFND/GW_CLIENT to validate OData behaviour

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting to confirm pushdown via ST05
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Some changes accidentally pull data to application server
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Capture an ST05 trace and verify generated SQL pushes WHERE, JOIN, and aggregates

cds-views-014.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Multi-layer testing approach for CDS views before production.

Used for:
- Catching defects in unit, integration, and authorization layers
- Validating OData behaviour for Fiori consumers
- Confirming HANA pushdown stays intact

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- ABAP Unit + CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT
- ADT Data Preview under different user IDs
- /IWFND/GW_CLIENT for OData, /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE for activation
- ST05 trace and /IWFND/ERROR_LOG

Interview keyword: unit + auth + OData + pushdown verification

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT for CDS view unit testing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT SQL test doubles create karta hai — database tables ke mock versions. Steps: cl_osql_test_environment=>create se environment create karo tables ki dependency list pass karke. insert_test_data use karke controlled test records se populate karo. Phir CDS view se SELECT karo — ye test doubles se read karta hai, real tables se nahi. CL_ABAP_UNIT_ASSERT se results assert karo. Teardown mein destroy call karo. Ye isolated, repeatable unit tests enable karta hai jo real business data ke bina run hote hain — CI/CD pipelines ke liye suitable.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT creates SQL test doubles — mock versions of database tables. Steps: create environment with cl_osql_test_environment=>create passing dependency list of tables. Use insert_test_data to populate with controlled test records. Then SELECT FROM the CDS view — it reads from test doubles, not real tables. Assert results with CL_ABAP_UNIT_ASSERT. Call destroy in teardown. This enables isolated, repeatable unit tests that run without real business data — suitable for CI/CD pipelines.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team had CDS view for complex document status derivation. Testing with real data was unreliable — data changed daily. Set up CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT with controlled test data covering all status combinations. 8 unit tests covering each status path. Tests ran in 2 seconds, no DB dependency. When CDS view logic changed, unit tests immediately caught regressions. Deployed automated test run in CI pipeline — runs on every transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Unit test mein real database data pe depend karna — 'test karo actual system pe.' Real data changes, tests become unreliable. CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT se controlled, isolated tests banao jo hamesha same result dein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
CLASS zcl_cds_unit_test DEFINITION FOR TESTING
  RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA env TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment.
    METHODS: setup, teardown,
             test_active_orders FOR TESTING.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_cds_unit_test IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD setup.
    env = cl_osql_test_environment=>create(
      VALUE #( ( 'VBAK' ) ( 'VBUK' ) ) ).
    env->insert_test_data( VALUE vbak_tt(
      ( vbeln = '0001' kunnr = 'C1' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD teardown. env->destroy( ). ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD test_active_orders.
    DATA lt TYPE TABLE OF zi_active_orders.
    SELECT * FROM zi_active_orders INTO TABLE @lt.
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial(
      act = lt msg = 'Should have results' ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test CDS view authorization (DCL) before production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DCL ke liye authorization testing ko multiple test user profiles chahiye: full authorization wala super user — verify karo ki saara data visible hai. Specific org unit access wala restricted user — verify karo ki sirf permitted data return ho. No-access user — verify karo ki empty result ya authorization exception aaye. SU01 use karo specific role assignments ke saath test users create/manage karne ke liye. Test karo ABAP SELECT ke through (sy-subrc check karo), /IWFND/GW_CLIENT ke through different user credentials ke saath, aur Fiori app simulation ke through. SUIM transaction dikhata hai ki kaunse authorization objects check ho rahe hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization testing for DCL requires multiple test user profiles: super user with full authorization — verify all data visible. Restricted user with specific org unit access — verify only permitted data returned. No-access user — verify empty result or authorization exception. Use SU01 to create/manage test users with specific role assignments. Test via ABAP SELECT (check sy-subrc), via /IWFND/GW_CLIENT with different user credentials, and via Fiori app simulation. SUIM transaction shows which authorization objects are checked.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: DCL testing scenario: Sales order CDS view restricted by sales organization. Three test users: User A with VKORG 1000, User B with VKORG 2000, User C with no sales authorization. Test results: User A sees only VKORG 1000 orders, User B sees only VKORG 2000 orders, User C gets authorization error. All three scenarios verified before QA transport. Found issue: User C got empty result instead of error — adjusted DCL to raise proper exception.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: DCL testing sirf apne admin user se karna — admin ke paas sab kuch access hota hai, DCL restrictions nahi dikhti. Hamesha low-privilege test users se test karo jo real end users simulate karein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test authorization via ABAP
REPORT ztest_dcl_auth.

" Test as restricted user (run with SU01 test user)
SELECT * FROM zi_salesorder
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orders).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  WRITE: / 'Records found:', lines( lt_orders ).
  " Verify only authorized sales orgs present
  LOOP AT lt_orders INTO DATA(ls_order).
    IF ls_order-SalesOrg <> 'USER_PERMITTED_VKORG'.
      WRITE: / 'SECURITY ISSUE - unauthorized data visible!'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ELSE.
  WRITE: / 'No records - authorization may be too restrictive'.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test OData service generated from CDS view using /IWFND/GW_CLIENT?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Transaction /IWFND/GW_CLIENT SAP mein built-in OData testing tool hai. Steps: transaction kholo, /sap/opu/odata/sap/SERVICE_NAME/EntitySet format mein service URL enter karo, HTTP method choose karo, optionally POST/PUT ke liye headers ya body add karo, Execute click karo. Response HTTP status code, response headers, aur response body (XML ya JSON) dikhata hai. Pehle $metadata request use karke entity types aur properties verify karo. $filter, $orderby, $top, $select parameters test karo. Response structure ko us se compare karo jo Fiori app expect karti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Transaction /IWFND/GW_CLIENT is the built-in OData testing tool in SAP. Steps: open transaction, enter service URL in format /sap/opu/odata/sap/SERVICE_NAME/EntitySet, choose HTTP method, optionally add headers or body for POST/PUT, click Execute. Response shows HTTP status code, response headers, and response body (XML or JSON). Use $metadata request first to verify entity types and properties. Test $filter, $orderby, $top, $select parameters. Compare response structure with what Fiori app expects.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Fiori app was returning no data. Investigation: OData URL tested in /IWFND/GW_CLIENT — HTTP 200 returned but empty collection. Investigated further with $filter removed — still empty. Checked DCL — found authorization check failing silently (with @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK, a DCL role that grants no access returns an empty result set instead of an error). Fixed DCL object. /IWFND/GW_CLIENT retested — data returned. Fiori app worked immediately. Without /IWFND/GW_CLIENT, would have debugged in browser network tab blindly.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf Fiori app se testing karna OData ki. Browser network tab mein raw OData response mushkil se padhna padta hai. /IWFND/GW_CLIENT se seedha backend test karo — cleaner response, easier debugging. Aur $metadata endpoint hamesha pehle test karo — service structure verify hoti hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" /IWFND/GW_CLIENT test URLs:

" 1. Service metadata - structure verify
GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/$metadata
" Response: all entity types, properties, navigation properties

" 2. Entity set - all records
GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/ZC_CustomerOrderSet

" 3. With filter
GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/ZC_CustomerOrderSet
    ?$filter=SoldToParty eq 'CUST001'
    &$select=SalesOrder,SoldToParty,NetValue
    &$top=10
    &$format=json

" 4. Single entity by key
GET .../ZC_CustomerOrderSet('0000012345')

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use ST05 SQL Trace to verify CDS view pushdown?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST05 SQL Trace ABAP session se executed saare SQL statements capture karta hai. CDS pushdown verify karne ke liye: ST05 trace activate karo, ABAP program ya Fiori action run karo, trace deactivate karo, display karo. CDS view name ke liye filter karo. Verify karo: saare joins aur conditions ke saath single SQL statement (multiple individual table queries nahi). Execution time check karo. 'Explain' button use karo HANA execution plan kholne ke liye — index usage, join strategies, no full table scans verify karo. Pushdown verified hota hai jab CDS logic ek single optimized HANA query ke roop mein appear karti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST05 SQL Trace captures all SQL statements executed from an ABAP session. To verify CDS pushdown: activate ST05 trace, run the ABAP program or Fiori action, deactivate trace, display trace. Filter for the CDS view name. Verify: single SQL statement with all joins and conditions (not multiple individual table queries). Check execution time. Use 'Explain' button to open HANA execution plan — verify index usage, join strategies, no full table scans. Pushdown verified when CDS logic appears as single optimized HANA query.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance analysis: CDS view for order list was slow. ST05 trace revealed: instead of one SQL, there were 500 individual SQL statements — N+1 problem. Developer had written ABAP loop that called SELECT FROM CDS view for each order header to get item count. Fix: redesigned CDS view with COUNT subquery. ST05 after fix: one SQL with subquery. Runtime dropped from 22 seconds to 1.2 seconds. ST05 essential for diagnosing such patterns.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: ST05 trace run karo aur just dekho ki queries run huin, bina analyze kiye kitni queries hain aur kya SQL generate hua. ST05 ka full value: count the statements, check the generated SQL quality, verify index usage via EXPLAIN. Sirf 'it worked' check karna enough nahi hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST05 testing procedure:
" 1. Transaction ST05 → Activate Trace
" 2. Run ABAP program:
REPORT ztest_cds_st05.
SELECT * FROM zi_salesorder
  WHERE SoldToParty = 'CUST001'
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data).
WRITE lines( lt_data ).
" 3. ST05 → Deactivate Trace → Display Trace
" 4. Filter: Object Name = ZI_SALESORDER
" 5. Expected: 1 SQL statement like:
"    SELECT ... FROM VBAK JOIN VBAP ON ...
"    WHERE KUNNR = 'CUST001'
" 6. NOT expected: multiple SELECT per record

" ST05 also shows:
" - Execution time per statement
" - Records returned
" - Use Explain button for execution plan

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test CDS views in Eclipse ADT data preview?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Eclipse ADT Data Preview development ke dauran CDS view output test karne ka sabse fast tarika hai. ADT mein CDS view source (.ddls file) kholo, F8 press karo. Data Preview saare view fields dikhata hai. WHERE conditions add karke filter karo, specific columns select karo, row limit set karo. Results grid format mein display hote hain — sortable, filterable. Input parameters wali CDS views ke liye, ADT preview execute karne se pehle parameter values ke liye prompt karta hai. Project Explorer mein CDS view pe right-click menu ke through bhi available hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Eclipse ADT Data Preview is the fastest way to test CDS view output during development. Open the CDS view source (.ddls file) in ADT, press F8. Data Preview opens showing all view fields. Add WHERE conditions to filter, select specific columns, set row limit. Results display in grid format — sortable, filterable. For CDS views with input parameters, ADT prompts for parameter values before executing preview. Also available via right-click menu on the CDS view in Project Explorer.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Development workflow: After writing CDS view with 3-way join, F8 preview showed NULL values in description field. Immediately identified: association condition was wrong — joining on wrong key field. Fixed in editor, saved, activated, F8 again — description now populated. Total debug time: 3 minutes. Without F8, would have written ABAP test program, run it, analyzed output — 15 minutes minimum.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Eclipse ADT install nahi karna aur sirf SE11/SE16N se test karna. ADT F8 preview bahut powerful hai — inline editing, instant feedback, no separate program needed. Agar ADT nahi hai development machine pe, request immediately — CDS development ADT ke bina very inefficient hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ADT Data Preview usage:
" 1. Open CDS view file in Eclipse ADT
"    (Project Explorer → Package → Data Definitions → ZCDS_VIEW)
" 2. Press F8
" 3. Data Preview toolbar options:
"    - Add filter (WHERE condition)
"    - Choose columns (SELECT specific fields)
"    - Set max rows
"    - Export to CSV/Excel

" For parameterized views:
" F8 shows parameter dialog first:
" Enter P_KeyDate: 20241231
" Then executes: SELECT FROM view( P_KeyDate = '20241231' )

" SE16N alternative (no Eclipse needed):
" Transaction SE16N
" Table name: ZI_SALESORDER (CDS view name)
" Max. no. of hits: 500
" Execute → same data grid

followupAnswerBank.question: What is transport testing strategy for CDS views?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS view transport strategy: Development transport mein DDLS (CDS source), DCLS (DCL role), aur SE11 SQL view entry shamil hote hain — teeno ko saath travel karna chahiye. QA mein, functional test (correct data return ho), authorization test (DCL per user role correctly restrict kare), performance test (ST05 trace acceptable execution plan verify kare), aur regression test (existing consumers abhi bhi work karen) execute karo. Transport notes mein document karo: kya change hua, test evidence, required authorizations. Production transport sirf QA sign-off ke baad. OData-exposed views ke liye, har system mein /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE mein service activate karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS view transport strategy: Development transport includes DDLS (CDS source), DCLS (DCL role), and SE11 SQL view entry — all three must travel together. In QA, execute functional test (correct data returns), authorization test (DCL restricts correctly per user role), performance test (ST05 trace verifies execution plan acceptable), and regression test (existing consumers still work). Transport notes should document: what changed, test evidence, authorizations required. Production transport only after QA sign-off. For OData-exposed views, also activate service in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE in each system.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Transport checklist we created after multiple incidents: 1) DDLS in transport, 2) DCLS in transport, 3) Any extended views in transport, 4) /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE activation documented, 5) QA test evidence attached (screenshots of F8 preview, /IWFND/GW_CLIENT test), 6) Authorization test evidence (tested with 3 user types), 7) ST05 trace confirming no performance regression. This checklist eliminated all CDS-related go-live issues for 6 months.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf CDS view transport karna aur QA mein sirf admin user se test karna. Production mein regular users ko authorization error milta hai kyunki DCL transport miss hua ya end-user role testing nahi ki. Always transport complete set, always test with end-user equivalent roles.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport request contents for CDS view deployment:
" Check via SE10 or Eclipse Transport Organizer

" Required objects in transport:
" R3TR DDLS ZI_CUSTOMERORDER      ← CDS view
" R3TR DCLS ZI_CUSTOMERORDER      ← DCL role
" R3TR TABU  IWFND entries          ← if OData activation needed

" Post-transport checklist in QA:
" [ ] SE16N: data preview working?
" [ ] F8 in ADT: data correct?
" [ ] /IWFND/GW_CLIENT: OData working?
" [ ] Test with user having full auth: data visible?
" [ ] Test with restricted user: only permitted data?
" [ ] Test with no-auth user: error or empty?
" [ ] ST05 trace: single SQL, no N+1 pattern?
" [ ] Regression: existing reports still working?

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test CDS views that use input parameters?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Parameterized CDS views ka testing: ADT F8 preview mein, parameter dialog values ke liye prompt karta hai — test values enter karo aur execute karo. ABAP test program mein, SELECT FROM view_name( param1 = value1 ) syntax use karo. Test scenarios: valid expected input (correct data verify karo), invalid input (no data ya proper error verify karo), boundary values (date ranges, limits pe amounts), initial/empty value (behavior verify karo — kuch views crash karte hain, kuch saara data return karte hain). OData ke liye, parameters ko function imports ki tarah ya URL parameters ki tarah test karo service definition pe depend karte hue.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing parameterized CDS views: In ADT F8 preview, parameter dialog prompts for values — enter test values and execute. In ABAP test program, use SELECT FROM view_name( param1 = value1 ) syntax. Test scenarios: valid expected input (verify correct data), invalid input (verify no data or proper error), boundary values (date ranges, amounts at limits), initial/empty value (verify behavior — some views crash, some return all data). For OData, test parameters as function imports or as URL parameters depending on service definition.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: CDS view ZI_OpenItems had input parameter P_KeyDate. Test plan: P_KeyDate = '20241231' (year-end, expect many records), P_KeyDate = '19000101' (historical, expect zero records), P_KeyDate = sy-datum (current date, should match live open items), P_KeyDate = '99991231' (future date, should show all items). Each scenario verified data correctness. Found bug: initial date '00000000' caused HANA error — added ABAP wrapper that defaults to sy-datum if initial passed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf happy path test karna — valid parameter se data aata hai, done. Edge cases miss hote hain: what if date is in future? What if empty string passed? What if special characters? Parameterized views ka robust testing zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Testing parameterized CDS view
" ADT F8: dialog shows - Enter P_KeyDate: [__________]
" Type: 20241231 → Execute

" ABAP test for multiple parameter values
REPORT ztest_param_cds.

" Test case 1: Valid past date
SELECT * FROM zi_openitems( P_KeyDate = '20241231' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_yearend).
WRITE: / 'Year-end open items:', lines( lt_yearend ).

" Test case 2: Future date (should return all open items)
SELECT * FROM zi_openitems( P_KeyDate = '99991231' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_future).
WRITE: / 'Future date items:', lines( lt_future ).

" Test case 3: Very old date (should return 0)
SELECT * FROM zi_openitems( P_KeyDate = '20000101' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_old).
WRITE: / 'Old date items:', lines( lt_old ).

" Assert lt_yearend <= lt_future (future should include all)

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE in CDS view testing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE (OData Service Maintenance) wahan hai jahan generated OData services SAP system mein register aur activate hote hain. @OData.publish: true wali CDS view activate hone ke baad, service backend mein exist karti hai par accessible nahi hoti jab tak yahan register na ho. Steps: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE kholo, Add Service, auto-generated service name search karo (usually CDS_VIEW_NAME_CDS), use add karo, activate karo, /IWFND/GW_CLIENT button ke through test karo. Har system mein — Dev, QA, Prod — alag se karna padta hai. Service URL format ban jaata hai /sap/opu/odata/sap/SERVICE_NAME/.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE (OData Service Maintenance) is where generated OData services are registered and activated in a SAP system. After CDS view with @OData.publish: true is activated, the service exists in backend but is not accessible until registered here. Steps: open /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, Add Service, search for auto-generated service name (usually CDS_VIEW_NAME_CDS), add it, activate it, test via /IWFND/GW_CLIENT button. Must be done in each system — Dev, QA, Prod — separately. The service URL format becomes /sap/opu/odata/sap/SERVICE_NAME/.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Typical Fiori project issue: Backend CDS view activated, OData service generated, but Fiori app showed 'Service not found'. Investigation: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE checked — service was created but not added and activated. Added service ZC_SALESORDER_CDS, activated, tested in /IWFND/GW_CLIENT — response 200 OK. Fiori app immediately worked. Classic setup step that new developers often skip.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Har system mein /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE activation karna bhool jana — transport ABAP objects carry karta hai but service activation is system-specific manual step (or scripted via SICF). In QA aur Production dono mein separately karna padta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE steps:
" 1. Transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE
" 2. Click 'Add Service'
" 3. System Alias: LOCAL (or gateway alias)
" 4. Technical Service Name: search 'ZC_CUSTOMERORDER'
" 5. Find: ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS (auto-generated name)
" 6. Select and Add
" 7. Activate ICF Node if prompted
" 8. Back to service list → Select service → Test
"    (opens /IWFND/GW_CLIENT automatically)

" Service URL after activation:
" https://host:port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/
" Test: append $metadata to verify structure
" https://host:port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_CUSTOMERORDER_CDS/$metadata

cds-views-015.easyUnderstanding: Interviews mein CDS Views ke kuch tricky questions puche jaate hain jo confuse karte hain. 'CDS aur ALV mein kya difference hai?' — ye comparison hi galat hai, dono ka kaam alag hai. 'Kya CDS ABAP programs replace kar sakta hai?' — nahi, sirf data access layer replace karta hai. Annotation ke bare mein bhi kuch trap questions hain. Ye jaanna important hai ki interviewer kya sunna chahta hai.
cds-views-015.hinglishMasterExplanation:
CDS Views interview trap questions ko samajhna zaroori hai:

**Trap 1: 'CDS aur ALV mein kya difference hai?'**
Ye comparison hi wrong hai — CDS data layer hai, ALV presentation layer hai. Interviewer check karta hai ki tum layer confusion mein toh nahi ho. Sahi jawab: 'Ye comparison apple aur orange ka hai — dono alag purposes ke liye hain. CDS database se data access karta hai, ALV us data ko UI mein display karta hai.'

**Trap 2: 'Kya CDS Views ABAP programs ko replace kar sakte hain?'**
Nahi — CDS sirf data access/modeling layer replace karta hai. Business logic, complex processing, file operations, ALV output — sab ABAP mein hi rehta hai. CDS ABAP ke andar ka SELECT replace karta hai, poora ABAP nahi.

**Trap 3: '@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #DISABLED — kab use karo?'**
#DISABLED exist nahi karta — valid options hain: #CHECK, #NOT_REQUIRED, #NOT_ALLOWED, #PRIVILEGED_ONLY. Interviewer test karta hai exact knowledge.

**Trap 4: 'CDS view SE11 table se kaisa alag hai?'**
SE11 table real data store karta hai, CDS view virtual definition hai — data access logic define karta hai, data store nahi karta. CDS is a VIEW, table is storage.

**Trap 5: 'Kya ek CDS view dusra CDS view use kar sakta hai?'**
Haan — ye CDS layering ka core concept hai. Interface view ko consumption view consume karta hai. CDS stacking supported aur recommended.

**Trap 6: 'DCL object kaunse system mein create hota hai?'**
Development system mein — same as CDS view, ADT mein, transport ke through promote hota hai. DCLS object type Eclipse ADT mein.

**Trap 7: 'CDS view mein WHERE clause aur HAVING mein kya difference hai?'**
WHERE non-aggregated rows filter karta hai, HAVING aggregated groups filter karta hai — same as standard SQL.
cds-views-015.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Interview mein CDS Views ke trap questions often wrong comparisons ya edge case knowledge test karte hain. Common trap: comparing CDS with ALV — these are different layers, not alternatives. CDS is the data access layer, ALV is the output presentation layer. Another trap: asking if CDS replaces ABAP — CDS replaces the data access patterns inside ABAP, not ABAP itself. Business logic, file operations, user interaction still need ABAP. For annotation traps: knowing exact annotation names and values matters — @AccessControl.authorizationCheck has specific allowed values: #CHECK, #NOT_REQUIRED, #NOT_ALLOWED, #PRIVILEGED_ONLY. Wrong values cause activation errors. For tricky DCL questions: DCL objects are ABAP Repository objects of type DCLS, created in ADT, transported same as CDS views, but are separate objects that must be explicitly included in transports.'

Hinglish tip: Trap questions ka best defense hai confident rehna aur agar nahi pata toh honestly bolna 'I am not sure about exact syntax, but the concept is...' Interviewer galat answer sunna nahi chahta — honest uncertainty better hai than wrong confident answer.
cds-views-015.codeExamples:
" Trap Q: What are valid values for @AccessControl.authorizationCheck?
" Answer - these are the valid values:
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK              " use DCL, check always
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED       " skip check (dangerous in prod)
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED        " view must not be accessed directly
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #PRIVILEGED_ONLY    " only privileged callers allowed

" Trap Q: Can a CDS view access another CDS view?
" Yes - this is normal layering:
define view ZC_ConsumptionView      " consumption view
  as select from ZI_InterfaceView   " uses another CDS view as source!
{ key ZI_InterfaceView.SalesOrder,
      ZI_InterfaceView.Customer }

" Trap Q: WHERE vs HAVING in CDS
define view ZI_AggregationDemo
  as select from vbak
{
  key vbak.kunnr as Customer,
  sum(vbak.netwr) as TotalValue
}
where vbak.audat >= '20240101'   " WHERE: filters before aggregation
group by vbak.kunnr
having sum(vbak.netwr) > 10000   " HAVING: filters after aggregation

" Trap Q: What is sqlViewName and is it mandatory?
" Old syntax: @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName required
" New syntax: DEFINE VIEW ENTITY - no sqlViewName needed
" Both are valid, newer projects prefer VIEW ENTITY
cds-views-015.interviewScriptHinglish: CDS views pe interview trap questions deep understanding test karte hain. CDS view versus ALV: ek data model hai, doosra UI control hai. CDS ABAP reports ko replace karta hai: read scenarios ke liye haan, transactional ya process logic ke liye nahi. Exact @AccessControl values hain #CHECK, #NOT_REQUIRED, #NOT_ALLOWED, aur #PRIVILEGED_ONLY. Associations lazy joins hain, eager nahi. CDS view versus CDS view entity: entity form modern DEFINE VIEW ENTITY syntax use karta hai built-in features ke saath aur koi separate SQL view name nahi hota. Do CDS views ek sqlViewName share nahi kar sakte. Function modules aur methods ko CDS ke andar call nahi kar sakte kyunki yeh declarative hai. EXTEND VIEW OO inheritance se differ karta hai. Business value senior-level understanding signal karna hai jo surface theory se aage jaata hai.
cds-views-015.interviewScriptEnglish: Interview trap questions on CDS views test deep understanding. CDS view versus ALV: one is a data model, the other a UI control. CDS replacing ABAP reports: yes for read scenarios, no for transactional or process logic. Exact @AccessControl values are #CHECK, #NOT_REQUIRED, #NOT_ALLOWED, and #PRIVILEGED_ONLY. Associations are lazy joins, not eager ones. CDS view versus CDS view entity: the entity form uses the modern DEFINE VIEW ENTITY syntax with built-in features and no separate SQL view name. Two CDS views cannot share an sqlViewName. Function modules and methods cannot be called inside CDS because it is declarative. EXTEND VIEW differs from OO inheritance. The business value is signalling senior-level understanding that goes beyond surface theory.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Equating CDS view with ALV control
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Mixes data layer with presentation; shows shallow understanding

cds-views-015.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Trap interview questions designed to expose surface knowledge of CDS views.

Used for:
- Senior interview preparation
- Demonstrating mature understanding of CDS
- Avoiding wrong-confidence answers

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- DEFINE VIEW vs DEFINE VIEW ENTITY
- @AccessControl values: #CHECK | #NOT_REQUIRED | #NOT_ALLOWED | #PRIVILEGED_ONLY
- Association = lazy JOIN, $expand triggered
- EXTEND VIEW vs OO inheritance

Interview keyword: precise definitions, no overclaiming

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the difference between CDS Views and ALV?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS Views aur ALV completely different layers pe operate karte hain aur ek doosre ke alternatives nahi hain. CDS data access layer hai — ye define karta hai ki data database level pe kaise fetch, join, aur model hota hai. ALV (ABAP List Viewer) presentation framework hai — ye ABAP programs mein tabular data display karta hai sorting, filtering, column selection jaise features ke saath. Inhe saath use karte hain: CDS view se data SELECT karke internal table mein laao, phir us internal table ko ALV se display karo. CDS raw SQL patterns replace karta hai; ALV output mechanism hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS Views and ALV operate at completely different layers and are not alternatives to each other. CDS is a data access layer — it defines how data is fetched, joined, and modeled at the database level. ALV (ABAP List Viewer) is a presentation framework — it displays tabular data in ABAP programs with features like sorting, filtering, column selection. They are used together: SELECT data FROM a CDS view into an internal table, then display that internal table using ALV. CDS replaces raw SQL patterns; ALV is the output mechanism.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project usage together: Inventory report uses ZI_InventoryStatus CDS view (joins MARA, MARD, MARC — material stock data). ABAP report selects data from CDS view into internal table. ALV displays the internal table with sorting by plant, color coding for low stock. CDS handled data model, ALV handled display. Neither replaces the other.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Interview mein ye answer dena ki 'CDS views ne ALV replace kar diya.' Ye completely wrong hai. CDS ne SELECT * FROM mara type raw SQL coding replace kiya hai — aur Fiori apps ke liye ALV ki zarurat nahi — lekin classical ABAP reports mein ALV aaj bhi standard hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" CDS + ALV together - not alternatives
REPORT zrep_cds_with_alv.

" Step 1: Fetch data via CDS view (data layer)
SELECT matnr, mtart, material_desc
  FROM zcds_material_desc  " CDS view does the join
  WHERE mtart = 'FERT'
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_materials).

" Step 2: Display via ALV (presentation layer)
DATA(lo_alv) = cl_salv_table=>factory(
  IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lo_table)
  CHANGING  t_table      = lt_materials ).
lo_table->display( ).  " ALV renders the output
" CDS = data, ALV = display — complementary layers

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: Can CDS Views replace ABAP programs completely?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS Views sirf data access aur modeling layer replace karte hain — ye ABAP programs ko replace nahi karte. ABAP abhi bhi chahiye: complex business logic processing, procedural workflows, file operations, spool output, form printing, external systems call karne, dialog programs ke through complex user interaction, BAPIs, RFCs, background jobs, aur bahut kuch ke liye. CDS replace karta hai: raw SELECT statements, SE11 database views, kuch SEGW OData kaam. Fiori apps ke liye, CDS + RAP kuch classical ABAP replace kar sakta hai, par complex custom logic ke liye abhi bhi ABAP classes ya function modules chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS Views replace only the data access and modeling layer — they do not replace ABAP programs. ABAP is still needed for: complex business logic processing, procedural workflows, file operations, spool output, form printing, calling external systems, complex user interaction via dialog programs, BAPIs, RFCs, background jobs, and much more. CDS replaces: raw SELECT statements, SE11 database views, some SEGW OData work. For Fiori apps, CDS + RAP can replace some classical ABAP, but complex custom logic still needs ABAP classes or function modules.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Customer question during training: 'If we use CDS for everything, can we eliminate ABAP?' Reality check: CDS-based Fiori app for purchase order creation uses CDS view for data display, but the actual save logic — validation, posting to EKKO/EKPO, workflow trigger, email notification, error handling — still requires ABAP (RAP behavior implementation). CDS is the data model, ABAP is the logic. You cannot send an email from a CDS view annotation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Interview mein zyada modernism dikhane ke chakkar mein ye bolna 'CDS ne ABAP replace kar diya in S/4HANA.' Interviewer immediately follow-up puche ga: 'Toh business logic kahan jaati hai?' Correct answer: 'CDS data access layer modernize karta hai, ABAP business logic layer pe rehta hai.'
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" What CDS replaces:
" OLD: SELECT * FROM mara INTO TABLE @lt_mat. ← raw SQL
" NEW: SELECT * FROM zi_materialdata INTO TABLE @lt_mat. ← CDS

" What CDS cannot replace (still needs ABAP):
" 1. Business logic
IF ls_order-netwr > 100000.  " ABAP condition
  CALL FUNCTION 'CREDIT_CHECK'.  " ABAP FM call
ENDIF.
" 2. File operations - only ABAP
OPEN DATASET '/data/file.csv' FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE.
" 3. Email sending - only ABAP
DATA(lo_mail) = cl_bcs=>create_instance( ).
" 4. Complex output - ABAP + SmartForms/Adobe Forms
" CDS = data model only, not program replacement

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the exact valid list of @AccessControl.authorizationCheck values?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: @AccessControl.authorizationCheck annotation ke valid values hain: #CHECK — authorization DCL role use karke check hoti hai, production ke liye recommended. #NOT_REQUIRED — koi authorization check perform nahi hoti, sirf development/testing ke liye, kabhi production mein nahi. #NOT_ALLOWED — view pe direct access allowed nahi hai, sirf dusre views consume kar sakte hain. #PRIVILEGED_ONLY — sirf privileged callers view access kar sakte hain. Koi #NONE ya #DISABLED value nahi hai — ye CDS activation errors cause karte. Annotation omit hone par default #CHECK hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The valid values for @AccessControl.authorizationCheck annotation are: #CHECK — authorization is checked using DCL role, recommended for production. #NOT_REQUIRED — no authorization check performed, only for development/testing, never production. #NOT_ALLOWED — direct access to the view is not permitted. #PRIVILEGED_ONLY — only privileged callers can access the view. There is no #DISABLED or #NONE value — these would cause CDS activation errors. Default when annotation is omitted is #CHECK.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Interview question: Candidate said @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #DISABLED is used for development. Interviewer asked to confirm — candidate doubled down. Interviewer tested: #DISABLED does not exist, activation error occurs. Candidate failed technical screen. Knowing exact valid values demonstrates real hands-on CDS experience. Easy to verify: Eclipse ADT content assist (Ctrl+Space) after #  shows valid enum values.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Annotation values guess karna ya remember karne ki koshish karna bina verify kiye. ADT mein Ctrl+Space content assist sahi values dikhata hai. Bina ADT ke: SAP Help documentation mein @AccessControl annotation page dekho. Wrong annotation values = immediate activation error = view does not work.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Valid @AccessControl.authorizationCheck values:

" #CHECK - standard production value
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZI_ProductionView ...
" Requires: DCL source DCLS object with same name

" #NOT_REQUIRED - dev/testing only
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
define view ZI_TestView ...
" No DCL needed, no auth check - NEVER in production!

" #NOT_ALLOWED - direct access to view is forbidden
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
define view ZI_RestrictedView ...
" View can only be consumed by other views, not directly

" #PRIVILEGED_ONLY - only privileged callers may access
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #PRIVILEGED_ONLY
define view ZI_PrivilegedView ...
" Reserved for internal/framework use

" INVALID - these do not exist (activation error!):
" @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #DISABLED  ← ERROR!
" @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NONE       ← ERROR!

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the difference between a CDS view and a CDS view entity?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DEFINE VIEW original CDS syntax hai jisko @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName annotation chahiye — ye CDS view aur underlying SE11 SQL view dono create karta hai. DEFINE VIEW ENTITY newer syntax (ABAP 7.55+) hai jisko sqlViewName ki zarurat nahi — ye pure ABAP-layer object hai bina corresponding SE11 SQL view ke. View entities newer features support karti hain jaise abstract entities, RAP ke liye draft entities, aur SELECT ke saath slightly different behavior rakhti hain. SAP S/4HANA 2022+ mein new development ke liye VIEW ENTITY recommend karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DEFINE VIEW is the original CDS syntax requiring @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName annotation — this creates both a CDS view and an underlying SE11 SQL view. DEFINE VIEW ENTITY is the newer syntax (ABAP 7.55+) that does not require sqlViewName — it is a pure ABAP-layer object without a corresponding SE11 SQL view. View entities support newer features like abstract entities, draft entities for RAP, and have slightly different behavior with SELECT. SAP recommends VIEW ENTITY for new development in S/4HANA 2022+.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Migration project for new S/4HANA 2023 implementation: Architect decision — all new CDS views use DEFINE VIEW ENTITY. Team initially confused why no sqlViewName annotation. Explained: VIEW ENTITY is the modern approach, no SE11 SQL view created, more aligned with RAP framework, draft handling built-in. Old views from 2019 implementation used DEFINE VIEW — both coexist in same system. Forward path is VIEW ENTITY.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Dono same hain — sirf syntax alag hai' kehna. VIEW ENTITY has real differences: no SQL view in SE11, different behavior in some SELECT scenarios, RAP draft support, and slightly different activation path. Not just syntax sugar.

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: How do associations work in CDS — are they JOINs or not?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Associations default mein JOINs nahi hain — ye lazily resolved declared relationships hain. Agar association sirf consumers ko expose hoti hai SELECT list mein association name list karke (e.g., _Customer), koi join nahi hota jab tak consumer explicitly path expression use karke access na kare. However, agar association fields directly view ke SELECT list mein projected hain (e.g., _Customer.name1 as CustomerName), tab view query hone pe join execute hota hai — association implicit join ban jaati hai. Best practice: associations ko consumers ke liye expose karo lazy resolution ke liye; sirf tab association fields project karo jab hamesha chahiye hon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Associations are not JOINs by default — they are declared relationships resolved lazily. If the association is only exposed to consumers by listing the association name in the SELECT list (e.g., _Customer), no join occurs unless the consumer explicitly accesses it using path expression. However, if association fields are directly projected in the view's SELECT list (e.g., _Customer.name1 as CustomerName), then a join is executed when the view is queried — the association becomes an implicit join. Best practice: expose associations to consumers for lazy resolution; only project association fields when always needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance investigation: CDS view had _Customer association. Developer added _Customer.name1 directly to SELECT list — every query now always joined KNA1 even when only order header was needed. Split into: ZI_OrderHeader (association exposed, not projected) and ZC_OrderWithCustomer (consumption view that projects _Customer.name1 for Fiori app that always shows customer name). Header report performance improved 3x — KNA1 join removed for pure header queries.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Association matlab JOIN nahi hota kabhi' kehna. Ye half-truth hai. When you project association's fields directly in SELECT list, join happens. Association stays lazy only when exposed as navigation property to consumer. This distinction matters for performance architecture.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Case 1: Association exposed - NO join by default
define view ZI_OrderLazy
  as select from vbak
  association [0..1] to kna1 as _Customer
    on $projection.kunnr = _Customer.kunnr
{
  key vbak.vbeln,
      vbak.kunnr,
      _Customer  " exposed as nav property - NO join unless consumer uses it
}
" Consumer: SELECT vbeln, kunnr FROM zi_orderlazy → NO join to KNA1
" Consumer: SELECT vbeln, _Customer.name1 FROM zi_orderlazy → JOIN to KNA1

" Case 2: Association fields projected - JOIN always happens
define view ZI_OrderEager
  as select from vbak
  association [0..1] to kna1 as _Customer
    on $projection.kunnr = _Customer.kunnr
{
  key vbak.vbeln,
      vbak.kunnr,
      _Customer.name1 as CustomerName  " projected! JOIN always executed
}

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What happens if two CDS views have the same @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName value SAP system mein globally unique honi chahiye — ye ek SE11 database view object name represent karti hai. Agar do CDS views same sqlViewName specify karti hain, doosri view duplicate SE11 view name indicate karne wali error ke saath activation fail kar degi. Ye do same name wali tables jaisa hi hai. Naming convention recommendation: sqlViewName CDS view name se derive karo — CDS view name lo, abbreviation rules apply karke 16 characters mein fit karo, uniqueness ensure karo. Example: CDS view ZI_CustomerSalesOrder_2024 → sqlViewName ZCUSTSALORD24V.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName value must be globally unique within the SAP system — it represents an SE11 database view object name. If two CDS views specify the same sqlViewName, the second view will fail activation with an error indicating duplicate SE11 view name. This is similar to two tables with the same name. Naming convention recommendation: derive sqlViewName from CDS view name — take CDS view name, apply abbreviation rules to fit within 16 characters, ensure uniqueness. Example: CDS view ZI_CustomerSalesOrder_2024 → sqlViewName ZCUSTSALORD24V.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team incident: Two developers created CDS views in parallel — ZI_MaterialData and ZI_MaterialDesc — and both used sqlViewName 'ZMATDATA_V'. When second developer tried to activate, error: 'ZMATDATA_V already exists as SE11 view'. Resolution: one renamed sqlViewName to 'ZMATDESC_V'. Lesson: communicate naming within team, maintain a naming registry for sqlViewName values to avoid conflicts.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: CDS view ko copy-paste karke new view banana aur sqlViewName change karna bhool jana. Two views same sqlViewName = one of them never activates. Always change BOTH the DEFINE VIEW name AND the @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName when copying.

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: Can you call a function module or method inside a CDS view?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS Views ABAP function modules, methods, ya classes call karna support nahi karte — ye SQL-based data definitions hain, ABAP programs nahi. Ye fundamental architectural constraint hai. CDS views use kar sakte hain: SQL built-in functions (CONCAT, SUBSTRING, CAST, UPPER, LOWER, COALESCE, CASE expressions), arithmetic operations, aggregate functions. Ye call nahi kar sakte: BAPI, FM, ABAP class methods, user-defined functions. Agar complex non-SQL logic chahiye, pattern ye hai: CDS view raw data provide karta hai, ABAP class/program CDS view consume karke business logic apply karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS Views do not support calling ABAP function modules, methods, or classes — they are SQL-based data definitions, not ABAP programs. This is a fundamental architectural constraint. CDS views can use: SQL built-in functions (CONCAT, SUBSTRING, CAST, UPPER, LOWER, COALESCE, CASE expressions), arithmetic operations, aggregate functions. They cannot call: BAPI, FM, ABAP class methods, user-defined functions. If complex non-SQL logic is needed, the pattern is: CDS view provides raw data, ABAP class/program consumes CDS view and applies business logic.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Design discussion: Business wanted CDS view to calculate 'preferred vendor score' using a complex algorithm. Developer suggested calling Z_CALC_VENDOR_SCORE FM from within CDS view. Not possible — CDS is SQL only. Solution: CDS view ZI_VendorBase provides raw vendor data. ABAP class ZCL_VendorScore calculates score: selects from ZI_VendorBase, applies scoring algorithm, stores in Z-table ZVENDOR_SCORE. Second CDS view ZI_VendorWithScore reads ZI_VendorBase and ZVENDOR_SCORE — joins via CDS. Two-step approach: logic in ABAP, result accessible via CDS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Main ek CASE statement se complex logic handle kar lunga CDS mein' sochna — CASE works for simple conditions but not procedural logic. FM calls, database commits, file reads, BAPI calls — none possible in CDS. Know the boundary: CDS = declarative SQL, ABAP = procedural logic.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" IMPOSSIBLE in CDS - no FM/method calls
" This does NOT work:
define view ZI_VendorScore_WRONG
  as select from lfa1
{
  key lfa1.lifnr,
  " CALL FUNCTION 'Z_CALC_SCORE'  ← SYNTAX ERROR in CDS!
  " ZCL_SCORE=>calculate( lifnr ) ← NOT POSSIBLE in CDS!
  lfa1.name1
}

" CORRECT pattern - ABAP does logic, CDS provides data
" Step 1: CDS provides raw data
define view ZI_VendorBase
  as select from lfa1
{ key lifnr, name1, land1 }

" Step 2: ABAP class calculates score, stores in Z-table
" ZCL_VENDOR_SCORER reads ZI_VendorBase, calculates, updates ZVSCORE

" Step 3: CDS joins base + score result
define view ZI_VendorWithScore
  as select from ZI_VendorBase
  left outer join zvscore on ZI_VendorBase.lifnr = zvscore.lifnr
{ key lifnr, name1, zvscore.score as VendorScore }

followupAnswerBank.question: Trap: What is the difference between CDS EXTEND VIEW and inheritance in OO ABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS EXTEND VIEW existing CDS view mein fields add karta hai bina base view modify kiye — ye additive extension hai, inheritance nahi. Tum existing fields override nahi kar sakte, unke types change nahi kar sakte, ya view ki logic alter nahi kar sakte. Ye SAP standard views mein custom fields (append structures se) upgrade-safe tarike se add karne ke liye use hota hai. ABAP OO inheritance method overriding, polymorphism, aur behavior modification allow karta hai. CDS extension purely additive hai — sirf fields. CDS mein method overriding ka equivalent nahi hai; tum nahi badal sakte ki existing fields kaise calculate ho rahe hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS EXTEND VIEW adds fields to an existing CDS view without modifying the base view — it is an additive extension, not inheritance. You cannot override existing fields, change their types, or alter the view's logic. It is used to add custom fields (from append structures) to SAP standard views in an upgrade-safe way. ABAP OO inheritance allows method overriding, polymorphism, and behavior modification. CDS extension is purely additive — fields only. There is no equivalent of method overriding in CDS; you cannot change how existing fields are calculated.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Customer requirement: Add custom field ZZPAYMENT_TERMS_TEXT to SAP standard I_SalesOrder view. Solution: EXTEND VIEW I_SalesOrder WITH ZX_SalesOrder_Payment — added ZZPAYMENT_TERMS_TEXT field from VBAK append structure. I_SalesOrder now shows custom field in all consumers. Not a modification — upgrade-safe extension. Could not change existing SalesOrder or Customer fields — only addition allowed. This is the correct extension approach for standard SAP CDS views.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: EXTEND VIEW se existing field logic change karne ki koshish karna — ye possible nahi hai. Agar existing field ka calculation change karna ho, consume that view in a new ZI_ custom view and override the field with new alias or CASE. Extension = add only, not modify.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" CDS EXTEND VIEW - additive fields only
extend view I_SalesOrder with ZX_SalesOrder_Custom
{
  " Adding custom fields from VBAK append structure
  vbak.zzpayment_notes    as PaymentNotes,
  vbak.zzregion_custom    as SalesRegion
  " Cannot change existing fields like SalesOrder, Customer
  " Cannot override calculation of existing fields
  " Only new field addition allowed
}

" vs ABAP OO inheritance - can override
CLASS zcl_child DEFINITION INHERITING FROM zcl_parent.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS get_name REDEFINITION.  " override parent method
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_child IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_name.
    " Different implementation than parent
    result = 'Custom Name'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
" CDS EXTEND = additive only; OO inheritance = override possible
